figures.caption: Figure 9. Qualitative comparison on Den-SOFT [47] dataset. Our method achieves the best results in both indoor and outdoor large-scale scenes.
Figure 5. Qualitative comparison of our results against the baselines on the FisheyeNeRF [12] dataset. Ours has more details, better texture, and fewer floaters.
Table 3. Performance of our method and four baselines on DenSOFT [47] dataset (Train View & Test View).
Figure 4. Schematic diagram of our camera association method.
Figure 3. Illustration of the 3DGS single-view training paradigm and our proposed cross-view joint optimization strategy.
Figure 10. Qualitative comparison of ablation studies on cross-view joint optimization (CVO) with fisheye or pinhole camera inputs.
